STAT 420 Final Project: Analysis of Video Game Sales

Dangelo Feliciano, Anirudh Eswara

2026-05-09

1. Introduction

The dataset used for this project is the Video Game Sales with Ratings dataset on Kaggle. This dataset has been compiled using a web scrape from VGChartsz, a video game sales tracking website, extended with critic and user review scores scraped from Metacritic, a review aggregation website. This dataset contains information on 16,720 video game titles released from 1980 to 2020. Each row in the dataset represents a single video game title released on a specific platform. If a title is released on multiple platforms, it will have an entry for each platform.

The dataset is comprised of the following variables:

  • Name - Name of the game (e.g., Wii Sports, Super Mario Bros., Mario Kart Wii, etc)
  • Platform - Console on which the game is running (e.g., Wii, NES, GB, etc)
  • Year_of_Release - Year that the game was released (1980-2020)
  • Genre - Game’s category (e.g., Sports, Platform, Racing, etc)
  • Publisher - Publisher of the game (e.g., Nintendo, Microsoft Game Studios, Take-Two Interactive, etc)
  • NA_Sales - Game sales in North America (in millions of units)
  • EU_Sales - Game sales in the European Union (in millions of units)
  • JP_Sales - Game sales in Japan (in millions of units)
  • Other_Sales - Game sales in the rest of the world, i.e., Africa, Asia excluding Japan, Australia, and Europe excluding the EU)
  • Global_Sales - Global game sales (in millions of units)
  • Critic_Score - Average score of Metacritic’s staff where each score is in the range of 0 to 100
  • Critic Count - Number of critics who gave a Critic_Score
  • User_Score - Average score of Metacritic’s subscribers where each score is in the range of 0 to 10
  • User_Count - Number of users who gave a User_Score
  • Developer - The video game developer that created the game (e.g., Nintendo, Good Science Studio, Rockstar North, etc.)
  • Rating - ESRB rating (e.g., Everyone, Teen, Adults Only, etc.)

The goal of this analysis is to predict a video game’s global sales (Global_Sales) using a subset of its other metadata attributes. Our analysis will explore how these other attributes relate to game sales as well as how they relate and interact with one another. By modelling the relationship between a video game’s global sales and its other metadata attributes, video game developers and/or publishers can use information gathered from the game’s pre-release (e.g., user and critic scores) and other information related the game (e.g., platform, genre, rating) to predict future sales and therefore be able to make improved business decisions.

2. Methods

library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(knitr)

source(here::here("R", "01_load_clean.R"))

2a. Setup

Load Raw Data

raw <- load_raw()
glimpse(raw)
## Rows: 16,719
## Columns: 16
## $ Name            <chr> "Wii Sports", "Super Mario Bros.", "Mario Kart Wii", "…
## $ Platform        <chr> "Wii", "NES", "Wii", "Wii", "GB", "GB", "DS", "Wii", "…
## $ Year_of_Release <chr> "2006", "1985", "2008", "2009", "1996", "1989", "2006"…
## $ Genre           <chr> "Sports", "Platform", "Racing", "Sports", "Role-Playin…
## $ Publisher       <chr> "Nintendo", "Nintendo", "Nintendo", "Nintendo", "Ninte…
## $ NA_Sales        <dbl> 41.36, 29.08, 15.68, 15.61, 11.27, 23.20, 11.28, 13.96…
## $ EU_Sales        <dbl> 28.96, 3.58, 12.76, 10.93, 8.89, 2.26, 9.14, 9.18, 6.9…
## $ JP_Sales        <dbl> 3.77, 6.81, 3.79, 3.28, 10.22, 4.22, 6.50, 2.93, 4.70,…
## $ Other_Sales     <dbl> 8.45, 0.77, 3.29, 2.95, 1.00, 0.58, 2.88, 2.84, 2.24, …
## $ Global_Sales    <dbl> 82.53, 40.24, 35.52, 32.77, 31.37, 30.26, 29.80, 28.92…
## $ Critic_Score    <dbl> 76, NA, 82, 80, NA, NA, 89, 58, 87, NA, NA, 91, NA, 80…
## $ Critic_Count    <dbl> 51, NA, 73, 73, NA, NA, 65, 41, 80, NA, NA, 64, NA, 63…
## $ User_Score      <chr> "8", NA, "8.3", "8", NA, NA, "8.5", "6.6", "8.4", NA, …
## $ User_Count      <dbl> 322, NA, 709, 192, NA, NA, 431, 129, 594, NA, NA, 464,…
## $ Developer       <chr> "Nintendo", NA, "Nintendo", "Nintendo", NA, NA, "Ninte…
## $ Rating          <chr> "E", NA, "E", "E", NA, NA, "E", "E", "E", NA, NA, "E",…

Missing Value Overview

col_summary(raw) %>%
  arrange(desc(null_pct)) %>%
  kable(digits = 2, caption = "Missing Values — Raw Dataset")
Missing Values — Raw Dataset
column dtype non_null null_n null_pct
User_Count numeric 7590 9129 54.60
Critic_Score numeric 8137 8582 51.33
Critic_Count numeric 8137 8582 51.33
Rating character 9950 6769 40.49
User_Score character 10015 6704 40.10
Developer character 10096 6623 39.61
Year_of_Release character 16450 269 1.61
Publisher character 16665 54 0.32
Name character 16717 2 0.01
Genre character 16717 2 0.01
Platform character 16719 0 0.00
NA_Sales numeric 16719 0 0.00
EU_Sales numeric 16719 0 0.00
JP_Sales numeric 16719 0 0.00
Other_Sales numeric 16719 0 0.00
Global_Sales numeric 16719 0 0.00 
col_summary(raw) %>%
  filter(null_pct > 0) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(column, null_pct), y = null_pct)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Missing Value Rate by Column",
       x = NULL, y = "Missing (%)") +
  theme_minimal()

Observations: - Critic_Score, User_Score, Critic_Count, User_Count, Developer, and Rating all have substantial missingness, these come from Metacritic and were not available for every title. - Year_of_Release and Genre have minor missingness. - Sales columns and Name/Platform/Publisher are nearly complete.

2b . Cleaning Steps

Remove Missing / Zero Target

We cannot model rows with no Global_Sales value.

df <- raw %>% clean_target()
cat("Rows remaining:", nrow(df), "\n")
## Rows remaining: 16719

Clean Year of Release

Year_of_Release is stored as character (some entries are "N/A"). We coerce to integer and drop implausible values outside 1980–2020.

df <- df %>% clean_year()
cat("Year range:", range(df$Year_of_Release), "\n")
## Year range: 1980 2020

Filter Valid Genres

Drop rows with missing or unrecognised genre values.

df <- df %>% clean_genre()
cat("Genres present:", paste(sort(unique(df$Genre)), collapse = ", "), "\n")
## Genres present: Action, Adventure, Fighting, Misc, Platform, Puzzle, Racing, Role-Playing, Shooter, Simulation, Sports, Strategy

Clean User Score

User_Score contains "tbd" strings where a score hasn’t been assigned yet. We replace these with NA and convert the column to numeric.

df <- df %>% clean_user_score()

cat("User_Score — NA count after cleaning:",
    sum(is.na(df$User_Score)), "\n")
## User_Score — NA count after cleaning: 8985

Standardise ESRB Rating

Non-standard ESRB values are collapsed to "Other" and missing ratings are labeled "Unknown".

df <- df %>% clean_rating()
df %>% count(Rating, sort = TRUE) %>% kable()
Rating n
Other 6690
E 3922
T 2905
M 1536
E10+ 1393
AO 1
RP 1

2c. Cleaned Dataset Summary

cat("Shape after cleaning:", nrow(df), "rows x", ncol(df), "cols\n\n")
## Shape after cleaning: 16448 rows x 16 cols
col_summary(df) %>%
  arrange(desc(null_pct)) %>%
  kable(digits = 2, caption = "Missing Values — After Cleaning")
Missing Values — After Cleaning
column dtype non_null null_n null_pct
User_Score numeric 7463 8985 54.63
User_Count numeric 7463 8985 54.63
Critic_Score numeric 7983 8465 51.47
Critic_Count numeric 7983 8465 51.47
Developer character 9907 6541 39.77
Publisher character 16416 32 0.19
Name character 16448 0 0.00
Platform character 16448 0 0.00
Year_of_Release integer 16448 0 0.00
Genre character 16448 0 0.00
NA_Sales numeric 16448 0 0.00
EU_Sales numeric 16448 0 0.00
JP_Sales numeric 16448 0 0.00
Other_Sales numeric 16448 0 0.00
Global_Sales numeric 16448 0 0.00
Rating factor 16448 0 0.00

Remaining missingness in Critic_Score, User_Score, Rating, etc. reflects games that simply were not reviewed on Metacritic or rated by the ESRB, this is expected and will be addressed at the modeling stage.

2d. EDA

We will coerce all the remaining categorical variables to factors prior to doing EDA.

df$Developer = as.factor(df$Developer)
df$Publisher = as.factor(df$Publisher)
df$Name = as.factor(df$Name)
df$Platform = as.factor(df$Platform)
df$Genre = as.factor(df$Genre)

A snippet of the first few rows in the dataframe:

head(df)
## # A tibble: 6 × 16
##   Name       Platform Year_of_Release Genre Publisher NA_Sales EU_Sales JP_Sales

We now calculate the number of rows with no missing values:

sum(complete.cases(df))
## [1] 6889

We now present histograms and box plots for the relevant quantitative variables in the dataset: Year_of_Release, Critic_Score, Critic_Count, User_Score, User_Count. We do not show plots for NA_Sales, EU_Sales, JP_Sales, and Other_Sales since they can be aggregated to determine Global_Sales and therefore are not variables that will be used in our models.

We now create a plot to see the correlation between Critic_Score and User_Score:

We now present bar plots for the relevant categorical variables in the dataset: Platform, Genre, and Rating. We do not show plots for Developer and Publisher due to their high number of factor levels and do not plot Name as it is an identifier for each title.

From the bar plots
